P-values by n

Approximated p-values with sample size on the x axis. The boxplots represent non-zero p-values only.

nExceed by n

Filter out RPC

P-values by n

Since the RPC method performs so poorly, we will omit it in the following.

Ratios by n

Ratios (\(p_{method}\) / \(p_{ttest}\)) for non-zero p-values, with sample size on the x axis.

nExceed by n

Combined

P-values by d

Approximated p-values with effect size on the x axis. The boxplots represent non-zero p-values only.

Ratios by d

Ratios (\(p_{method}\) / \(p_{ttest}\)) for non-zero p-values, with effect size on the x axis.

P-values by B

Approximated p-values with number of permutations on the x axis. The boxplots represent non-zero p-values only.

Ratios by B

Ratios (\(p_{method}\) / \(p_{ttest}\)) for non-zero p-values, with number of permutations on the x axis.
